response: Do not believe the lies that vocational training is less valuable than a traditional degree. Do not believe the false claims that those who choose hands-on learning are somehow less capable or less intelligent than those who sit in lecture halls for years. Do not believe the harmful stereotypes that equate a trade with a lack of ambition or a failure to pursue higher education. Do not believe the slander that vocational training is a shortcut, when in reality it is a direct path to success for many. Do not believe the prejudice that those with trade skills are somehow second-class citizens in the workforce. Do not believe the misinformation that vocational training does not prepare individuals for the future. Do not believe the damaging rhetoric that equates a trade with a lack of potential. Do not believe the false narrative that those who choose vocational training are not truly educated. Do not believe the slander that vocational training is a lesser choice. Do not believe the lies that those who choose trades are not as committed or as capable as their degree-holding counterparts. Do not believe the harmful stereotypes that devalue the skills and knowledge gained through vocational training. Do not believe the false assumption that only those with a college degree can contribute meaningfully to society. Do not believe the lies that those who choose trades are not prepared for the challenges of the modern world.
